summary: Iteration 2 frontend UI review at https://field-data-viewer.preview.emergentagent.com (390x844). Login screen with animated LoginScene renders correctly (sun, clouds, birds, mountains, turbines, plants, solar panels, water strip) and the dark navy card is present with ENVIROLYTICS + SUSTAINABILITY · PRIVATE · LIMITED, 'Sign in to your account' heading, email/password inputs, orange 'Sign Me In' pill, Policies link, VERSION 1.0 · SECURE LOGIN footer. Login with admin@envirolytics.com / Admin@Envirolytics2026 navigates to /dashboard. Bottom tab bar shows FIVE tabs in the correct order: Dashboard / Devices / Reports / Alerts / Profile (all tappable via get_by_role('tab', ...)). Reports tab (testID reports-screen) renders three cards: Borewell Consumption (range-7/30/90 chips + TOTAL pill + 2 borewell rows for HTTPS_SMOKE_FM_1782927309 and MAPTEST_FM_001, TOTAL 0 KL — real data from prod), Hourly Pumping (2 select-fm chips + pumping-chart + Total pumped · 0 KL over 24h footer), and Compliance Exports (all four buttons dl-fm-csv/dl-fm-pdf/dl-dwlr-csv/dl-dwlr-pdf). Tapping dl-fm-csv shows the ActivityIndicator spinner briefly and then writes 'Saved envirolytics_flowmeter_data_2026-07-03.csv' into download-note — download flow works end-to-end. Range-30 chip toggles active (green border/text) and re-fetches. Alerts tab renders Limit Breaches + Offline Devices sections. Profile tab shows Envirolytics Admin / Lucknow HQ / 26.8467 / 80.9462 / ADMIN. Logout returns to /login and login-form + scene SVG are visible again. CRITICAL: The Device Detail screen (/device/[id]) throws a red-screen 'ReferenceError: chartMeta is not defined' for BOTH Flowmeter and DWLR devices — the entire detail view is unusable (all detail-* testIDs invisible, back button gone, no FLOW RATE/WATER LEVEL chart, no metric tiles, no download buttons). This is a hard regression from iteration 1 where device detail worked. Root cause: `chartMeta` (used at app/device/[id].tsx:277, 289, 290, 297, 309, 312) is never declared — only exists as a StyleSheet key `chartMeta` at line 556. Additionally, `chartData` is hard-coded to `return []` for DWLR (line 109), so the promised WATER LEVEL chart cannot render even once chartMeta is fixed. Deprecated packages detected: none. SafeAreaView issues detected: none.

ui_bugs.issue: RED SCREEN — ReferenceError: chartMeta is not defined. `chartMeta` is used at lines 277, 289, 290, 297, 309, 312 but never declared inside the DeviceDetail component (only a StyleSheet key `chartMeta` exists at line 556, which is unrelated). Tapping any device card (Flowmeter or DWLR) crashes the whole detail screen — none of detail-hardware-id / detail-metric-flow / detail-metric-totalizer / detail-metric-battery / hours-6/24/72/168 / detail-download-csv / detail-download-pdf / FLOW RATE / WATER LEVEL are rendered. Fix: add a useMemo returning { title, color, unit } based on `kind` ('flowmeter' → { title:'FLOW RATE', color: colors.eco, unit:' m³/h' }; 'dwlr' → { title:'WATER LEVEL', color: colors.water, unit:' m' }).

`chartData` at lines 108–121 short-circuits with `if (kind !== 'flowmeter') return [];` — even after fixing chartMeta the DWLR Water-Level chart will always be empty. Extend the memo so DWLR maps history to { t: readingTs(r), v: pickReadingValue(r, ['water_level','level','depth']) } (reversed if descending — check API order).

integration_issues.issue: Unreachable due to chartMeta ReferenceError — the entire screen crashes on mount so no API integration can be exercised from the UI.

action_items: URGENT: Declare `chartMeta` inside DeviceDetail (app/device/[id].tsx). Suggested implementation: `const chartMeta = useMemo(() => kind === 'dwlr' ? { title: 'WATER LEVEL', color: colors.water, unit: ' m' } : { title: 'FLOW RATE', color: colors.eco, unit: ' m³/h' }, [kind]);` placed above the return().
Extend chartData memo in app/device/[id].tsx (lines 108–121) to build the series for DWLR (map water_level/level/depth) so the Water Level chart actually renders history.

Optional cleanup: the operator-precedence issue on setLastSeen (lines 72 and 87) was flagged in iteration_1 and is still present — wrap `o?.last_seen` in parentheses so the ternary evaluates correctly for offline devices.
critical_code_review_comments: chartMeta reference bug is a straightforward compile-time miss that should have been caught by TypeScript — but the file is being consumed via Metro's dev bundling without a full tsc pass. Consider running `npx tsc --noEmit` in CI before shipping.
chartData memo does not support DWLR at all, meaning the WATER LEVEL chart card advertised in the review request cannot be exercised even when the red screen is fixed.

success_rate.frontend: 5/8 scenarios PASS (login scene, login flow, five-tab bar, Reports full flow incl. dl-fm-csv + range-30, Alerts, Profile+logout). 3 scenarios BLOCKED by device-detail red screen: Flowmeter detail, DWLR detail, and any chart interaction.

seed_data_creation: None — used the production admin account (admin@envirolytics.com) as instructed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Prod account is admin@envirolytics.com / Admin@Envirolytics2026 against monitor.envirolytics.in. Preview URL is https://field-data-viewer.preview.emergentagent.com. Tabs are DOM-role='tab' — use page.get_by_role('tab', name='Reports'). The Reports screen's dl-fm-csv actually writes a real CSV via Web downloadAndShare (returns 'Saved envirolytics_flowmeter_data_YYYY-MM-DD.csv' inside download-note testID). BLOCKER for device-detail retest: fix chartMeta ReferenceError in app/device/[id].tsx first, otherwise every device-card tap will show 'Uncaught Error / chartMeta is not defined' modal.
rca_of_issue: In app/device/[id].tsx the JSX at lines 277/289/290/297/309/312 references a symbol `chartMeta` that is nowhere declared in the DeviceDetail component scope. The only `chartMeta` in the file is a StyleSheet key at line 556 (`chartMeta: { color: colors.textMuted, ... }`) which is completely different. On first render React tries to read `chartMeta.title` and throws ReferenceError, unwinding the whole DeviceDetail tree — hence a full red screen. Reproduction: log in, tap any device card (e.g. device-card-MAPTEST_FM_001 or device-card-MAPTEST_DWLR_001) → red 'Uncaught Error' overlay appears immediately. Mitigation: declare chartMeta as `const chartMeta = useMemo(() => kind === 'dwlr' ? { title: 'WATER LEVEL', color: colors.water, unit: ' m' } : { title: 'FLOW RATE', color: colors.eco, unit: ' m³/h' }, [kind]);` before the JSX return, and also extend chartData to build the DWLR series so the Water Level chart renders.
